# smrti

> Graph-based memory for AI agents, built on PostgreSQL + pgvector.

## Overview

smrti is a Rust library that provides graph-based storage and retrieval for AI agent memory. It is a dumb storage layer — no built-in LLM calls or embedding generation. Callers provide pre-computed data. Event-sourced, multi-tenant, fully configurable.

Three distribution targets:
- **smrti-core** (Rust crate): `cargo add smrti-core`
- **smrti** (Python package via PyO3, planned): `pip install smrti`
- **konf-smrti** (Konflux integration, planned): in-process workflow tools

Two API layers:
- **Memory** (Layer 1): flat params, string IDs, JSON returns with `_meta`
- **StorageProvider** (Layer 2): typed params, UUID, Rust structs

## Install

```toml
[dependencies]
smrti-core = "0.1"
```

Requires PostgreSQL 15+ with pgvector extension.

## Memory API (Layer 1 — LLM-friendly)

```rust
// Lifecycle
Memory::connect(config) -> Result<Memory>
memory.close() -> Result<()>

// Write (batch transactions — single commit per call)
memory.add_nodes(nodes, namespace) -> Result<Value>     // with optional embedding + model_name
memory.add_edges(edges, namespace) -> Result<Value>
memory.get_or_create(content, node_type, node_key, namespace) -> Result<Value>
memory.update_node(node_id, content, metadata, node_type) -> Result<Value>
memory.retract_node(node_id) -> Result<Value>
memory.retract_edge(edge_id) -> Result<Value>
memory.merge_nodes(keep_id, remove_id) -> Result<Value>

// Search (three modes: vector, text, hybrid)
memory.search(query_vector, text_query, namespaces, mode, ...) -> Result<Value>
memory.traverse(node_id, depth, edge_types, max_nodes) -> Result<Value>
memory.aggregate(edge_type, namespace, metadata_key, ...) -> Result<Value>
memory.get_edges(node_id, direction, edge_types) -> Result<Value>

// Session State (working memory, NOT event-sourced)
memory.state_set(key, value, session_id, namespace, ttl_seconds) -> Result<Value>
memory.state_get(key, session_id, namespace) -> Result<Value>
memory.state_delete(key, session_id, namespace) -> Result<Value>
memory.state_clear(session_id, namespace) -> Result<Value>
memory.state_list(session_id, namespace) -> Result<Value>
memory.state_prune_expired(namespace) -> Result<Value>

// Import/Export
memory.export_events(after_id, namespace, limit) -> Result<Value>
memory.import_events(events) -> Result<Value>

// GDPR
memory.purge_namespace(namespace) -> Result<Value>

// Scoped context
memory.scoped(namespace) -> ScopedMemory
```

All returns include `_meta` with operational info (duration_ms, event_ids, search_modes_used, etc.).

## Search Modes

- `mode="vector"` — cosine similarity via pgvector (requires query_vector)
- `mode="text"` — PostgreSQL full-text search with trigram fallback (requires text_query)
- `mode="hybrid"` — both, merged with Reciprocal Rank Fusion (requires both)

## Configuration (SmrtiConfig)

Key fields (all have defaults):
- `dsn` (required): PostgreSQL connection string
- `search_mode`: "hybrid" | "vector" | "text" (default: "hybrid")
- `search_language`: PostgreSQL text search config (default: "english")
- `search_limit`: default max results (default: 10)
- `distance_metric`: "cosine" | "l2" | "inner_product" (default: "cosine")
- `rrf_k`: RRF fusion constant (default: 60)
- `hnsw_m`: HNSW index connections (default: 16)
- `hnsw_ef_construction`: HNSW build quality (default: 64)
- `text_search_trigram_fallback`: enable trigram fallback (default: true)
- `session_state_default_ttl`: default TTL for session state (default: none)

## Concepts

- **Nodes**: entities/facts with content, type, metadata, optional embeddings
- **Edges**: typed directed relationships with temporal validity. NOT unique by (source, target, type) — multiple edges of same type allowed (A1 principle).
- **Events**: append-only log. Source of truth. Nodes/edges are projections.
- **Namespaces**: tenant isolation on all operations.
- **Session State**: ephemeral KV store per session. NOT event-sourced.

## Error Handling

All errors include context. Types: SmrtiError (Connection, Migration, Event, NodeNotFound, EdgeNotFound, Validation, Search, Embedding, Namespace, Database).

## Production Features

- All SQL uses parameterized `.bind()` — zero string interpolation
- Batch transactions via `apply_events_batch` — single commit per bulk write
- Recursive CTE for graph traversal — single query, no round-trips
- Advisory locking for atomic get_or_create
- Configurable trigram fallback for high-volume deployments
- GDPR purge with audit trail
- OpenTelemetry via feature flag (tracing crate)
